“I recently rescued my dog from a Chicago shelter and wanted to know which breeds she is. I also wanted to know if she had any health conditions. The information from the dna and health portion of the test was interesting and useful.”

content.p: Knowing your dog’s genetic makeup empowers you to tailor training, nutrition, and care to their unique needs —helping you ensure a happier, healthier, snugglier pup for years (and years) to come.

q.name: p 1
p 1
Give $20, Get $20
Instagram Facebook Twitter YouTube
Cat Genetics DNA Test NOT AVAILABLE YET
Do you want to discover more about what makes your furry companion the way they are? Feline DNA test…

content.p: Do you want to discover more about what makes your furry companion the way they are? Feline DNA testing analyses your kitty’s genetic makeup to provide insight about their ancestry, breed groups they belong to, similarity to wild cats and much more. You will not need to visit the vet because the test only requires a cheek swab sample! Results are available in 8 to 10 weeks from receipt of samples at the laboratory.

q.name: How does Cat Genetic testing work? When your cat’s cheek swab sample arrives at the laboratory, scie…

content.p: How does Cat Genetic testing work?

When your cat’s cheek swab sample arrives at the laboratory, scientists will commence testing by extracting the DNA using a chemical extraction process and then proceed to read and record the feline’s unique genetic code through the use of different sequencing machines. The laboratory’s avant- garde sequencing technology allows scientists to take an extensive look at your pet’s genetic data and look at hundreds of thousands of variants to discover more information about the cat’s ancestors. All the variants are then compared to the laboratory’s large database of highly sequenced pedigreed cats so that the most genetically similar individual or groups of purebred cats can be identified.

q.name: What can be discovered through Cat DNA testing? Based on the laboratory database, cat breeds are bro…

content.p: What can be discovered through Cat DNA testing?

Based on the laboratory database, cat breeds are broken down into three different categories: Western breeds (from Europe or the Americas), Eastern breeds (from Asia) and Hybrid breeds (from wild- domestic crosses). In the test report, your mixed-breed cat will more dominantly belong to one of these three groups. Within the dominant breed group, breeds are then ranked in the order of genetic similarity, the first breed being the one your cat is most genomically similar to of all the breeds. Why don’t you take a look at the full list of cat breeds tested? As the laboratory database expands, more breeds will be added!

q.name: Chat Live to our DNA Consultant

content.p: Chat Live to our DNA Consultant

q.name: p 1
p 1
Resources DNA Sample Collection Understanding Your Results

content.p: DNA Sample Collection Understanding Your Results

q.name: The report also comes with a chromosome painting, a beautiful visual representation of your cat’s ge…

content.p: The report also comes with a chromosome painting, a beautiful visual representation of your cat’s genetic similarities which makes the results easier to read and understand. The chromosome map displays regions on your cat’s genome that are similar to pedigreed cat breeds from the laboratory’s database. The chromosomes will be marked with distinct colours to help you visualize where each section of the genetic code relates to the tested breeds, which will then allow you to see which breed group influenced that specific section.

The feline DNA test also identifies your cat’s similarity to different wild cats such as the cheetah, leopard and cougar! The cat DNA test report includes a wild cat index percentage which indicates how genomically similar your cat is to wild relatives. This percentage is calculated by comparing your cat’s genome to that of other cats from the laboratory’s database, big and small. The number of similarities your cat’s DNA shares with different wild cats is recorded and then ranked in similarity to the wild relatives against all other domestic cats. As more cats are tested, the wild cat index percentage value may change in later versions of the report.

q.name: What about Sample Collection and Turnaround Time? This test requires a cheek swab sample from your c…

content.p: What about Sample Collection and Turnaround Time?

This test requires a cheek swab sample from your cat which is relatively simple and easy to collect. Simply use the special swab provided in the Cat Genetics test kit and carefully swirl it around your cat’s mouth for a few seconds as explained in the instructions printed inside your kit.
Results for this cat DNA test are available within 8 to 10 weeks from receipt of samples at the laboratory!

q.name: Other animal DNA testing kits available EasyDNA offers a variety of DNA testing services for canine,…

content.p: Other animal DNA testing kits available

EasyDNA offers a variety of DNA testing services for canine, feline, equine and avian which can be used by professional breeders, veterinarians and pet owners interested in discovering more about the parentage, heritage and health of their pet. For instance, Dog Allergy testing makes managing dog allergies easier, Dog Breed testing identifies your dog’s breed composition and Cat PKD testing determines if a cat is genetically predisposed to suffer from an inherited disorder known as polycystic kidney disease (PKD). Check out our complete range of tests and contact us directly with any questions.

q.name: Frequently Asked Questions Does this DNA test identify the breed of my cat? The Cat Genetics DNA tes…

content.p: Does this DNA test identify the breed of my cat?

The Cat Genetics DNA test is not a breed test and does not determine the breed of your cat but rather it identifies the breeds which your cat is most similar to. The first breed listed in your report is the breed which your cat is closest to genomically. It is important to note that even mixed-breed cats of unknown origin (polycat cats) are closer to some breeds more than others.
How does this test differ from other Genetic tests?

Many genetic tests screen only for what is already known meaning that as scientists make discoveries about new genes and traits one would need to have their cat’s DNA tested again and again with each new finding. Unfortunately, there is little known about cat genetics and much more left to be discovered. Unlike similar tests, the Cat Genetics test sequences your cat’s DNA including those DNA parts which the laboratory still doesn’t know anything about. Your cat’s genetic data is then saved and as more discoveries are made about what these unknown genes are doing, we will be able to tell you more and more new things about your furry friend.

q.name: Does your cat carry the gene that causes Polycystic Kidney disease?

content.p: Does your cat carry the gene that causes Polycystic Kidney disease?

content.p: Our leading genetic testing panel for dog breeders includes 200+ genetic health tests, now including prcd-PRA and CEA, along with traits and genetic diversity.

q.name: Powered by Wisdom PanelTM, Optimal SelectionTM Canine provides breeders with the most comprehensive…

content.p: Powered by Wisdom PanelTM, Optimal SelectionTM Canine provides breeders with the most comprehensive test of its kind. We’ve taken the latest scientific research on dog population genetics and developed a simple and easy at–home swab test that screens for multiple diseases and traits while also evaluating genetic diversity. This kind of testing can provide advantages over traditional techniques such as pedigree analysis and help breeders to develop proactive, sustainable breeding programs.

p 1
Canine HealthCheck Kit Retail: $189.95 Web Price: $179.95 Special Price: $149.95 The Canine HealthC…

content.p: The Canine HealthCheck is an easy to use gene#c screen of more than 250 di>erent inherited diseases and traits. Common, inherited diseases include those that a>ect metabolism, bones, eyes, kidneys and many more. You will receive results for each gene tested as normal, carrier, or at-risk for the diseases. q.name: Connect With Us !"#$
The Canine HealthCheck® is a screen of >250 genes that cause diseases or traits in dogs. This is not…

content.p: The Canine HealthCheck® is a screen of >250 genes that cause diseases or traits in dogs. This is not a diagnos#c test. Any abnormal results (carrier or a>ected), or any results that concern you, should be con[rmed using a diagnos#c test. Diagnos#c tes#ng is available from Paw Print Gene#cs® and in many cases, can be performed using exis#ng DNA from the Canine HealthCheck.
The Canine HealthCheck is not a breed iden#[ca#on test.

q.name: CAT HEALTH SCREEN This is great value profile that includes dozens disease and trait tests in a sing…

content.p: This is great value profile that includes dozens disease and trait tests in a single test. The Cat Health Screen also include a personalized LifePlanTM and a schedule you may follow with your veterinarian to implement a customized wellness plan for your cat.
Note: This is not a breed iden./ca.on test - you will need to know the breeds that make up your cat to oder this product

q.name: Description This test is designed for purebred cats, the domestic short hair (DSH) cat or the domest…

content.p: This test is designed for purebred cats, the domestic short hair (DSH) cat or the domestic long hair (DLH) cat. This great value test will screen your cat for dozens genetics diseases, traits and identify your cat's blood group.

Breed-specific diseases will be highlighted on the report. The additional health risk analysis will reveal the most relevant health risks for your pet based on their genetics, breed, age, weight, gender, location and lifestyle.
The Cat Health Screen also include a personalized LifePlanTM and a schedule you may follow with your veterinarian to implement a customized wellness plan for your cat.

q.name: How Do I Get My Results? Results are presented in an interactive online account where you can consta…

content.p: How Do I Get My Results?

Results are presented in an interactive online account where you can constantly update your pet’s records such as weight, body condition, nutrition, health records and medication. Your account will also give you access to special promotions and attractive offers via your very own personalized shop. You can easily share your pet’s profile with your veterinarian, health care providers, family and friends.

Collect your cat's DNA sample by gently swabbing the inside of their cheek for 2-5 seconds.
Secure your sample within the return shipping box and mail it back to our lab. Free return shipping too!

q.name: CHROMOSOME MAP AND BREED GROUPS Let Their Genome Do the Talking What part of your cat genome is simi…

content.p: What part of your cat genome is similar to

a Western Maine Coon? An Eastern Burmese? Or a perfectly adorable polycat? Using our world-class feline genomic database, Basepaws can estimate your tabby’s origins to more than 21 breed types and 4 breed regions.

q.name: A HEALTHY CAT IS A HAPPY CAT A Healthy Cat is a Happy Cat Your cats won't tell you when they're sick…

content.p: A HEALTHY CAT IS A HAPPY CAT
Your cats won't tell you when they're sick. We test for 39 genetic markers, reporting on mutation carrier status and predisposition to 17 diseases.
Polycystic Kidney Disease (PKD) Hypertrophic cardiomyopathy (HCM) Retinal degeneration

q.name: WILD CAT INDEX Your Cat's Wild Side You’ll find out how similar your cat's genome is to a Tiger, Lio…

content.p: You’ll find out how similar your cat's genome is to a Tiger, Lion, Cheetah or a Cougar as we compare and learn more about big cats. It may explain your kitten’s roar-like meow.

comment.p: 4/8/21, 1:33 AM, merged with p 1, Click here for an update on our COVID-19 response and mail delays. Thanks for the service and for Ha…

q.name: Your dog’s unique DNA DNA My Dog's simple cheek swab DNA test lets you learn the breeds in your dog…

content.p: DNA My Dog's simple cheek swab DNA test lets you learn the breeds in your dog and gain insight into the unique genetic background of your dog including the history of their breed, personality traits, exercise levels, and so much more!

Every dog has its own unique DNA. DNA My Dog provides you with a kit and very simple instructions for swabbing the cheeks of your dog and collecting your dog's DNA samples. The test is easy, painless and takes under a minute. After we receive your samples, they are processed and run through DNA My Dog’s extensive database of certified dog breeds. All DNA matches from the unique cheek cells of your dog are recorded and you get to discover the breeds
found in your dog’s unique canine heritage. It’s that easy!
After we get your DNA results we divide them up for you based on DNA matching tests. We provide you with percentages of the breeds found in your dog. Click here to order.

q.name: Your purchase of DNA My Dog test kits have raised over $97,000 for Canine Rescue to date! Details an…

content.p: Your purchase of DNA My Dog test kits have raised over $97,000 for Canine Rescue to date! Details
and shelters in our North American Shelter program can be found here. Sign up for updates and special offers!

q.name: Guess My Breed? Plus You Get Even More DNA My Dog also provides you with a custom certificate with…

content.p: DNA My Dog also provides you with a custom certificate with a photo of your dog stating their unique DNA composition. This one of a kind certificate is suitable for framing, and when you see it you will want to do just that! You get your results in less than 2 weeks.

Your results also include a personalized analysis of the breeds found in your dog from our database of the most popular common breeds. This handy reference has every level of all breeds found in your dog’s DNA genetic profile PLUS it lists the unique personality traits and genetic health concerns associated with each breed.
Act today. Order your test and see the true heritage of your dog in about two weeks.
This is Penny. Please select which breed you think she is.
Level 1 Staffordshire Bull Terrier Level 3 Boxer
Click here to learn what each level means.

q.name: DISCOVER! Your dog’s breed composition. Unique personality traits of your dog. Genetic health concer…

content.p: Your dog’s breed composition. Unique personality traits of your dog. Genetic health concerns. Predisposition to disease.
A custom photo certificate of the breeds found in your dog’s genetic breed composition. A percentage breakdown of the levels found in your dog’s unique DNA.
A report on dominant breeds, personality traits and health concerns.

Trace your dog’s ancestry back to their great-grandparents

q.name: p 1
Optimal SelectionTM Feline $99.99 Our leading genetic testing panel for both pedigreed and mixed-bre…

content.p: Our leading genetic testing panel for both pedigreed and mixed-breed cats includes 40+ genetic health tests, plus blood type, along with traits and genetic diversity.

Powered by Wisdom PanelTM, Optimal SelectionTM provides breeders with the most comprehensive test of its kind. We’ve taken the latest scientific research on cat population genetics and developed a simple and easy at–home swab test that screens for multiple diseases and traits while also evaluating genetic diversity. This kind of testing can provide advantages over traditional techniques such as pedigree analysis and help breeders to develop proactive, sustainable breeding programs.
Results for over 40 genetic diseases, reported in terms of known relevance to the specific breed evaluated.
Testing for 20+ traits including coat colors, coat types, and morphology.
Genetic diversity information for the tested cat, and the overall breed population Continuously updated research and breed information based on new discoveries

Optimal SelectionTM DNA service for cats is a genome-wide genetic analysis that gives information on several inherited characteristics. All received DNA test results are reported online to your user account in the secured Optimal SelectionTM database.

q.name: Included Health Tests Acute Intermittent Porphyria (Variant 1) Acute Intermittent Porphyria (Variant…

content.p: Acute Intermittent Porphyria (Variant 5) - NEW! Autoimmune Lymphoproliferative Syndrome Burmese Head Defect
Chediak-Higashi Syndrome (Discovered in Persian cats) - NEW! Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia
Earfold and Osteochondrodysplasia (Discovered in the Scottish Fold) Factor XII Deficiency (Variant 1)
Familial Episodic Hypokalaemic Polymyopathy (Discovered in Burmese) Glutaric Aciduria Type II - NEW!
GM2 Gangliosidosis, type II (Discovered in Burmese cats) - NEW!
GM2 Gangliosidosis, type II (Discovered in domestic shorthair cats) GM2 Gangliosidosis, type II (Discovered in japanese domestic cats) Hemophilia B (Variant 1)
Mucopolysaccharidosis Type VI (G1558A variant) - NEW! Mucopolysaccharidosis Type VI (T1427C variant) Mucopolysaccharidosis Type VII
Progressive Retinal Atrophy (Discovered in Bengal cats)
Progressive Retinal Atrophy (Discovered in Persian cats) - NEW! Pyruvate Kinase Deficiency

content.p: Gloves (Birman - White feet) White Spotting/Dominant White
Glitter coat - NEW and EXCLUSIVE to Wisdom Health Hairlessness (Discovered in Sphynx)
Rexing (discovered in Cornish Rex and German Rex) Rexing (discovered in Devon Rex)
Short tail (discovered in Japanese Bobtail cats) Short tail (discovered in Manx), two variants Polydactyly (mutation HW)

q.name: How is Orivet different from all other dog DNA tests? Orivet's proprietary algorithm is the only pro…

content.p: How is Orivet different from all other dog DNA tests?

Orivet's proprietary algorithm is the only product on the market that provides a true and complete clinical risk analysis for your pet based not only their DNA results but also on many other factors including: breed, lifestyle, age, gender, weight and geographic location.

q.name: What does this Premium Package include? The premium dog testing package includes a combination of th…

content.p: What does this Premium Package include?
The premium dog testing package includes a combination of three easy-to-use canine tests which are:
Below please find a brief description of each one of these tests.

Dog Allergy testing tests the proteins within a dog’s system to identify their reaction to more than 100 allergens as listed HERE, covering not only common foods and supplements but also daily household products which may result in being harmful to the dog. The test also provides helpful instructions to assist the dog owner in obtaining suitable foods, products and other items that their dog’s immune system shows no allergic reaction to. By learning the dog’s reaction to certain allergens, the owner will be able to change and improve multiple aspects of their life, being it the dog’s diet, accessories or home environment.

Using just a cheek swab sample, Dog Breed DNA testing analyses the dog’s unique genetic makeup to determine their breed mix. The breed composition is identified by referring to a database containing the DNA profiles of 95 of the most prevalent breeds of dogs found in mixed breeds dogs. In addition to the full breed composition, this test provides a report on the personality traits, exercise routines and behaviours to expect from each individual breed. A step by step guide of the illnesses and health problems that could plague the dog’s particular breed mix is also included!

Dog Genetic Age testing also requires a cheek swab sample and identifies a dog’s age at a genetic level by measuring the length of their telomeres, the protective caps at the end of each chromosome that tend to shorten with age. Biological age gives insight into the health situation, and predicted longevity of the tested dog. For instance, a dog which had a life of medical issues might have a higher than average biological age. Through this test, dog owners are able to determine the dog’s actual age when it is not known, take control of the ageing process by carrying out lifestyle or dietary changes and recognise signs of potential health risks such as heart disease.
All of the above dog tests are being offered for the discounted price of $195. In addition, the results for this premium package are available within only 2 to 3 weeks and sent by email!
Please take into account that turnaround time starts from the moment samples reach the laboratory.

Embark offers the most accurate and highest rated test on the market.

q.name: REVIEWS BREEDS HEALTH % It has been a long time since I have felt this much anticipation.…

content.p: It has been a long time since I have felt this much anticipation. Waiting for our rescue pup's DNA results, I felt like a child waiting for my birthday to arrive. We appreciated all the emails leading up to her health and breed info as well. Knowing her breeds has helped us to understand her temperament and to approach training, games and life wit...Read More

I did the DNA testing on our loving, family dog as a surprise for my wife's birthday. Being a rescue we often wondered aloud about his lineage, so ordering the kit, performing the swab & receiving the email of results was exciting. I didn't open the email, instead allowing her to be the first to do so on her birthday, surrounded by family. It ...Read More
Love Embark, but wish they dug further

Had a great experience with Embark and love all the services the DNA tests have to offer! Embark was great about updating you as to the status of the results. My only qualm is that our pup came back with 30% "Supermutt" and I couldn't help but feel like, well yes, that is what I'm paying you to find out!
Finding Talula's breed was my objective when I did Embark. But I got steps for my bed and couch based on her genetics to prevent injury to her spine so I can keep her living longer.
I bought my fur baby in lockdown, but as he was growing people were saying he was too big for a Cavapoo!
I have never owned a dog before and even though I asked all the questions with the breeder i.e health issues etc I was beginning to have doubts.
So I bought Embark DNA and health check. I was so impressed, they email you all through the...Read More
Worth the money and the wait

comment.p: 4/8/21, 7:19 PM, merged with p 1, Embark Breed + Health Kit $199 Or 3 interest-free payments of $67 with . Learn More !8724 Revie…

q.name: p 1
Be proactive about your cat's health Your cats won't tell you when they're sick. We test for genetic…

content.p: Be proactive about your cat's health
Your cats won't tell you when they're sick. We test for genetic markers, reporting on mutation carrier status and predisposition to different diseases.

q.name: Our home-based saliva collection kit is all you need to send your cat's DNA to our lab. All from hom…

content.p: Our home-based saliva collection kit is all you need to
send your cat's DNA to our lab.
All from home. No blood. Completely non-invasive – just a small saliva sample, for both Health and Breed tests. Free shipping both ways within the continental U.S.!
Collect your cat's DNA sample by gently swabbing the inside of their cheek for 5 seconds.
Create an account at basepaws.com and register your CatKit upon its arrival.
Secure your sample within the return shipping box and mail it back to our lab.

comment.p: Test will provide relatives, pedigree, or parent confirmation results.
Test will provide results on breed groups, wild counterparts, and/or regional information.
Information related to the company's accreditation.
An expert, celebrity, or other outside source providing approval or support of the genetic test company or pet genetic testing.
Test will provide health-related information such as genetic predisposition to certain diseases, carrier status of potentially harmful alleles, and drug sensitivity.
Statements instructing consumers how to collect samples, submit samples, check results, or register kits.
Company advertising the advantages of using their product(s) for consumers to learn about their pet.

Company advertises the tests as offering idiosyncratic information about a consumer’s pet. Examples include personalized genetics, customized wellness plans that are derived from the advertised product and the pet's individual genetic make-up.
Test will provide results on a pet’s phenotype or physical characteristics such as coat color, ear shape, etc.
Professional photograph of a domestic dog as a marketing tool.
Professional photograph of a domestic cat as a marketing tool.
Professional photograph of a domestic dog or cat and human model interacting as a marketing tool.
Materials and/or instructions detailing how test samples are processed and how consumers will receive their results.
Company provides detailed information about the genetic test and its associated features to include the kit, results, and other customizable features offered with the product.
Company provides a link to another subpage and/or a "promo code" for the consumer to get a discounted rate.
Information or links instructing consumers how to purchase the product or a simple statement about the purchase price.
Information about additional products and/or benefits included with purchasing a genetic test from the particular company. These are services in addition to test results.

Company encourages consumers to distribute product information to the consumer's associated contacts. This can be in the form of a link to share a post to one's personal social media account or the company advertising test results formatted to be sharable.
Link or form for consumer to enter contact information for the company to send them additional information. Also includes links to social media for consumers to follow the genetic company.
Information promoting the science for their tests and how the results are derived. These are also statements about how the company's technology is distinct from competitors.

Any indicator that consumers can trust the company and their services. Examples include companies stating they care about their customers, assurance test results have merit, offer quality services, and other qualities to convey consumers can be confident in the company and its products.
Informative video clip on genetic company web page to provide some sort of product information or demonstrate how consumers process their kits (e.g., collect sample, send in for analysis).
Statements about the use of their products to promote health and happiness of pet owners and/or pets.
Test will sequence the pet’s entire genome.
